title: Battenberg cake
subtitle: Checkerboard sponge in pink and vanilla, wrapped in marzipan
excerpt: Battenberg cake: a 2×2 checkerboard of vanilla and pink almond sponge, stuck together with apricot jam, and wrapped in a thin layer of marzipan. The sponge is baked in a divided tin, cut into four long strips, assembled, and rolled. Unmistakably British.
sourceNotes: Battenberg cake is documented in British baking from the late nineteenth century, reputedly created for the 1884 marriage of Prince Louis of Battenberg to Princess Victoria of Hesse. Mrs Beeton's later editions document the cake, and it became a British baking staple throughout the twentieth century. The standard form, a 2×2 checkerboard of vanilla and pink sponge wrapped in marzipan, is widely reproduced in British domestic baking references and is fully public-domain.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the sponge the day before. Assemble with jam and marzipan on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, 30 to 35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean

glossaryTerms.definition: Assembling a Battenberg requires two long sponge strips in each colour and patience with the marzipan. Trim each baked sponge to the same width and height, then cut each into two equal strips. Lay one vanilla and one pink strip next to each other, then stack with the colours alternating to form a 2×2 checkerboard. Use warm apricot jam as the glue between each strip and layer. To wrap: roll the marzipan to a rectangle that exactly fits around the four sides of the loaf. Brush the outside of the assembled sponge with jam, place it at one end of the marzipan, and roll up, pressing to seal the join at the base.

content.text: Battenberg is more assembly job than baking challenge. The sponge recipe is simple. The work is in the trimming: identical widths and heights on all four strips are essential for the checkerboard to work. Use a ruler.

items.prepNote: pink or red, for half the batter
warmed and sieved, for the glue and coating

content.text: Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Line a 900 g loaf tin with a foil divider down the centre, creating two equal halves. Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} until pale. Add the {{eggs}} one at a time. Fold in the {{self-raising-flour}} and {{almond-extract}}. Divide the batter equally between two bowls. Colour one half with {{food-colouring}}. Spoon each batter into one half of the prepared tin. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool completely.
Trim, assemble with jam and marzipan

method. Trim each sponge to the same height and width using a ruler. Cut each into two equal strips. Warm the {{apricot-jam}} and use it to glue the strips into a 2×2 checkerboard. Dust a surface with {{icing-sugar}} and roll the {{marzipan}} to a rectangle large enough to wrap the four sides of the loaf. Brush the outside of the assembled sponge with jam and roll tightly in the marzipan, seam-side-down.
